content.problemStatement:
Chef is trying to invent the light bulb that can run at room temperature without electricity. So he has NNN gases numbered from 000 to N−1N - 1N−1 that he can use and he doesn't know which one of the NNN gases will work but we do know it.

Now Chef has worked on multiple search algorithms to optimize search. For this project, he uses a modulo-based search algorithm that he invented himself. So first he chooses an integer KKK and selects all indices iii in increasing order such that i mod K=0i \bmod K = 0imodK=0 and test the gases on such indices, then all indices iii in increasing order such that i mod K=1i \bmod K = 1imodK=1, and test the gases on such indices, and so on.

Given NNN, the index of the gas ppp that will work, and KKK, find after how much time will he be able to give Chefland a new invention assuming that testing 111 gas takes 111 day.

For example, consider N=5,p=2N = 5, p = 2N=5,p=2 and K=3K = 3K=3.


On the 1st1^{st}1st day, Chef tests gas numbered 000 because 0 mod 3=00 \bmod 3 = 00mod3=0.
On the 2nd2^{nd}2nd day, Chef tests gas numbered 333 because 3 mod 3=03 \bmod 3 = 03mod3=0.
On the 3rd3^{rd}3rd day, Chef tests gas numbered 111 because 1 mod 3=11 \bmod 3 = 11mod3=1.
On the 4th4^{th}4th day, Chef tests gas numbered 444 because 4 mod 3=14 \bmod 3 = 14mod3=1.
On the 5th5^{th}5th day, Chef tests gas numbered 222 because 2 mod 3=22 \bmod 3 = 22mod3=2.
So after 555 days, Chef will be able to give Chefland a new invention


Input Format

The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains three space-separated integers NNN, ppp, and KKK.


Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing one integer — after how much time Chef will be able to give Chefland a new invention assuming that testing 111 gas takes 111 day.
content.inputFormat:
The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains three space‑separated integers N, p and K.
- N: total number of gases (indexed from 0 to N‑1)
- p: index of the working gas (0 ≤ p < N)
- K: the modulo value used by Chef's search algorithm
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing one integer – the day (starting from 1) on which gas p is tested. Testing one gas takes exactly one day.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For each test case we compute full = N/K and rem = N%K, then apply the formula answer = q*full + min(q,rem) + (a+1). The resulting days are 2, 4, 9 and 8 respectively.
N=5, K=3 → full=1, rem=2. p=2 gives q=2, a=0. sumPrev = 2*1 + min(2,2)=4. Answer = 4 + 0 + 1 = 5.
K > N, so full=0, rem=7. p=0 → q=0, a=0. sumPrev=0. Answer = 0 + 0 + 1 = 1 (the first day).

solution.approach:
For each remainder r (0 ≤ r < K) Chef tests all indices i such that i mod K = r in increasing order. The number of indices with remainder r among 0 … N‑1 is:
    cnt(r) = floor(N / K) + (r < N % K ? 1 : 0)
Let full = floor(N / K) and rem = N % K.
Let q = p % K (the remainder group of p) and a = floor(p / K) (its position inside that group, 0‑based).
All groups with remainder smaller than q are completely processed before reaching group q, contributing:
    sumPrev = q * full + min(q, rem)
Inside group q, p is the (a‑th) element, so we need a+1 days for this group.
Answer = sumPrev + (a + 1).
The formula is O(1) per test case and works for all constraints.
Edge cases (K > N, rem = 0, etc.) are naturally handled by the same formula.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work per test case.

code.Python3:
import sys

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = int(next(it)); p = int(next(it)); K = int(next(it))
        full = N // K
        rem = N % K
        q = p % K
        a = p // K
        sumPrev = q * full + min(q, rem)
        ans = sumPrev + a + 1
        out.append(str(ans))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
